title: Scams
description: Practice your understanding of scams and how to recognize them.

assessment_items.question_content:
Phishing scams are tricky because they try to fool you into giving away your personal information. 

**Which of the following is an example of a phishing scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an email from your favorite online store offering a big sale on clothes
a text message from a number you don't recognize, saying you won a gift card and need to click a link to claim it
a flyer in your mailbox advertising a local pizza place's special online order deal

assessment_items.question_content:
Phishing scams are attempts by scammers to trick you into giving out personal information.

**Which of the following is an example of a phishing scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: receiving an email from your favorite online store with a link for a special sale
getting a text message from your bank asking you to confirm your account number and password
signing up for a social media account

assessment_items.question_content:
Being aware of phishing scams can help you protect your personal information.

**Which of the following is an example of a phishing scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an email from your school asking you to sign up for a club
a pop-up ad on a website offering you a free gift if you enter your email address
a friend asking you to take a quiz on a popular app

assessment_items.question_content:
Pyramid schemes can be disguised as legitimate opportunities, but they usually focus on making money for the folks on top of the pyramid.

**Which of the following is an example of a pyramid scheme?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a company that pays you a commission for selling makeup to your friends and family
a company that asks you to pay a fee to join and promises you'll make money by getting friends and family to join as well
a company that asks that you use your own car to make deliveries

assessment_items.question_content:
Pyramid schemes can be disguised as legitimate opportunities, but they usually focus on making money for the folks on top of the pyramid.

**Which of the following is an example of a pyramid scheme?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: sharing a post on social media to help a friend sell their handmade jewelry
joining a group where you must recruit new members and pay a fee to earn money
a friend asking you to buy products they are selling

assessment_items.question_content:
Pyramid schemes can be disguised as legitimate opportunities, but they usually focus on making money for the folks on top of the pyramid.

**Which of the following is an example of a pyramid scheme?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a friend invites you to a party where you can buy discounted clothing
you are offered a job where you must pay a fee to start and earn money by recruiting new members
a family member asks you to donate money for a charity event

assessment_items.question_content:
Check cashing scams often involve fake checks and electronic payments.

**Which of the following is an example of a check cashing scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: getting a check from a company as payment for a freelance job you completed
cashing a check from your bank after closing a savings account
a person bought an item you are selling online, accidentally sent you too much money, and is asking for refund of the overpayment

assessment_items.question_content:
Check cashing scams often involve fake checks and electronic payments.

**Which of the following is an example of a check cashing scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: receiving a check after selling a product online
receiving a check as a birthday gift from a relative
receiving a check for sweepstakes you won, asking you to cover the cost of taxes and processing

assessment_items.question_content:
Check cashing scams often involve fake checks and electronic payments.

**Which of the following is an example of a check cashing scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: receiving a check after winning a contest you entered, asking you to deposit within $180$ days
receiving a check as a birthday gift from a relative, but asking that you give half to your sibling
being asked to cash a check for someone you don't know, keep $\$20$ and send the rest back

assessment_items.question_content:
It's important to be aware of potential scams when it comes to home renovation projects. 

**Which of the following is an example of a home renovation scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a contractor asking for your email to send you a detailed estimate
a contractor asking that you purchase materials before they start work
a contractor offering a steep discount if paid in cash upfront

assessment_items.question_content:
Scammers can use different tactics to take advantage of homeowners. 

**Which of the following is an example of a home renovation scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a contractor who asks you to obtain the building permit
a contractor asking that you purchase a certain brand of materials
a contractor who goes door-to-door offering to fix problems they notice on your home

assessment_items.question_content:
Knowing the warning signs of a scam can help protect you and your home.

**Which of the following is an example of a home renovation scam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a contractor who only accepts checks as payment
a contractor who guarantees their work for a set period of time
a contractor who goes door-to-door offering to fix problems they notice on your home
